content: Add test case: assertValid("read<U8>()", 100, "100"). This might take some plumbing.

[Session context summary — replaces earlier history]
The goal was to extend a minimal Tuff-to-TypeScript compiler to support reading typed values from stdin, arithmetic expressions with arbitrary chaining, and variable declarations/references. Initially, `index.ts` only returned source unchanged. I implemented a full tokenizer (supporting identifiers, numbers, `<`, `>`, `(`, `)`, `+`, `:`, `=`, `;`), a recursive descent parser building an AST of expressions (`ReadExpr`, `NumberLit`, `BinOp`, `IdentRef`) and statements (`LetDecl`, `ExprStmt`), and a code generator that tracks stdin token consumption indices. The compiler treats the final un-terminated expression as the program's return value, while prior semicolon-terminated statements execute for side effects or declarations. A two-pass generation approach collects declared variable names first to enforce name resolution at compile time, throwing on undefined references (which also correctly rejects invalid syntax like bare identifiers). `index.test.ts` was updated with seven test cases covering empty programs, syntax errors, single/multi-token stdin reads, chained addition, and let-declaration usage. TypeScript strict mode issues were resolved using non-null assertions for bounded string/array indexing, and an ESLint `no-case-declarations` violation was fixed by wrapping switch case bodies in braces. A PMD CPD duplication warning between nearly identical `expectToken`/`expect` methods was eliminated by consolidating them into a single parameterized method. All tests pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly with zero errors, and linting/Copy-Paste Detection checks now succeed. No open tasks remain; the compiler correctly handles all specified features within the current test suite.
